h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: "She were late yesterday." I'd always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like a rule than a mistake
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to frame the dialect form as a personal error to be fixed for the essay.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue has been progressively sharpening the distinction between statistical significance and practical value; the expected next move is a further refinement of the break-even arithmetic.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large. That frame made me skip the slower bit, sorry: what happens after the damage, with the loose material being taken away.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe the better way is: the flood makes the sharp bare scar, but the river line and gravel show the clearing that keeps coming back.
scored.naiveTrajectory: Before L3, the learner was moving toward separating sudden events from slow removal, but still framing the insight as a correction of an earlier mistake; L2 had already begun naming the split. The expected continuation would be to elaborate that split or apply it to the overlook, not to return to the initial 'flood doing everything' frame and reinterpret it as a cognitive block.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was basically starting from “he probably didn’t do it.”
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that is the procedural meaning
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward simply adopting the tutor's corrected wording while still struggling with the word 'innocent' sounding factual; the expected next turn would be a note revision that still grapples with that persistent feeling.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turn sought guidance on how far to extend the trophic cascade, suggesting a forward, additive elaboration of the answer.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I guess it did not leave the cup.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns set up an expectation that the learner will continue relying on visual disappearance as the primary criterion for salt being 'gone,' possibly resisting or not fully integrating the scale evidence.
